speaker: VALENTINE
text: If the Duke continue these favors towards

speaker: VALENTINE
text: you, Cesario, you are like to be much advanced. He

speaker: VALENTINE
text: hath known you but three days, and already you

speaker: VALENTINE
text: are no stranger.

speaker: VIOLA
text: You either fear his humor or my negligence, that

speaker: VIOLA
text: you call in question the continuance of his love. Is

speaker: VIOLA
text: he inconstant, sir, in his favors?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: No, believe me.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I thank you.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Here comes the Count.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Who saw Cesario, ho?

speaker: VIOLA
text: On your attendance, my lord, here.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Stand you awhile aloof.—Cesario,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Thou know’st no less but all. I have unclasped

speaker: ORSINO
text: To thee the book even of my secret soul.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Therefore, good youth, address thy gait unto her.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Be not denied access. Stand at her doors

speaker: ORSINO
text: And tell them, there thy fixèd foot shall grow

speaker: ORSINO
text: Till thou have audience.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Sure, my noble lord,

speaker: VIOLA
text: If she be so abandoned to her sorrow

speaker: VIOLA
text: As it is spoke, she never will admit me.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Be clamorous and leap all civil bounds

speaker: ORSINO
text: Rather than make unprofited return.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Say I do speak with her, my lord, what then?

speaker: ORSINO
text: O, then unfold the passion of my love.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Surprise her with discourse of my dear faith.

speaker: ORSINO
text: It shall become thee well to act my woes.

speaker: ORSINO
text: She will attend it better in thy youth

speaker: ORSINO
text: Than in a nuncio’s of more grave aspect.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I think not so, my lord.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Dear lad, believe it;

speaker: ORSINO
text: For they shall yet belie thy happy years

speaker: ORSINO
text: That say thou art a man. Diana’s lip

speaker: ORSINO
text: Is not more smooth and rubious, thy small pipe

speaker: ORSINO
text: Is as the maiden’s organ, shrill and sound,

speaker: ORSINO
text: And all is semblative a womans part.

speaker: ORSINO
text: I know thy constellation is right apt

speaker: ORSINO
text: For this affair.—Some four or five attend him,

speaker: ORSINO
text: All, if you will, for I myself am best

speaker: ORSINO
text: When least in company.—Prosper well in this

speaker: ORSINO
text: And thou shalt live as freely as thy lord,

speaker: ORSINO
text: To call his fortunes thine.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I’ll do my best

speaker: VIOLA
text:
To woo your lady.
                 
                Aside
                .
               Yet a barful strife!

speaker: VIOLA
text: Whoe’er I woo, myself would be his wife.
